title: CLAUDE.md
text: Four user-facing data functions expose internal datasets, plus citation/discovery helpers: - get_enrollment() — school enrollment (counts gross rates), backed enrollment_kang_fgv + enrollment_tertiary - get_schooling() — mean years schooling, backed schooling_kang_fgv - get_expenditure() — public expenditure education (% GDP, per-student % GDP per capita, “double ratio” indicators), backed expenditure_kang_fgv - get_progression() — grade-progression indicators (GDR6), backed progression_kang_fgv - educabr_cite() — builds bibentry/APA/BibTeX citations source key source column values - list_sources() — tibble every entry source vocabulary (discovery counterpart educabr_cite()) get_*() functions return tibbles canonical tidy-long schema: one row per observation, alternative sources separate rows (never separate columns), aggregations encoded explicit "total" factor levels (never NA). get_*() accepts core args (year, geo_level, geo, source, wide, lang) plus theme-specific filters.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: schema lives inst/dict/schema.yaml. defines required columns, controlled vocabularies (factor levels), primary-key columns, year domain. R/utils-schema.R provides load_schema() validate_against_schema() — every ETL script must call latter usethis::use_data(). Supporting dictionaries: - inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml — source keys + citation metadata (drives educabr_cite()) - inst/dict/vocabularies/indicators.yaml — indicator key registry - inst/dict/i18n.yaml — PT-BR label translations applied lang = "pt"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: get_enrollment() calls .load_enrollment_panel(), iterates .enrollment_datasets() (registry dataset names), fetches package namespace, fills missing optional columns .ENR_DEFAULTS, row-binds single canonical frame. New enrollment datasets must registered .enrollment_datasets(). pattern repeated themes (.load_schooling_panel() / .schooling_datasets(), .load_expenditure_panel() / .expenditure_datasets(), .load_progression_panel() / .progression_datasets()) — theme registry contributing dataset names.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Five datasets bundled, across four themes: - enrollment — enrollment_kang_fgv (BR+UF, 1871-2010, Kang/FGV), enrollment_tertiary (BR, 1907-2024, multi-source) - schooling — schooling_kang_fgv (BR+region+UF, 1925-2015, Walter & Kang) - expenditure — expenditure_kang_fgv (BR, 1933-2010, Kang & Menetrier) - progression — progression_kang_fgv (BR+20 UFs, 1955-2010, Kang/Paese/Felix)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Scripts follow five-step pattern: READ → TIDY → VALIDATE → ANNOTATE → WRITE. Run devtools::load_all() active educabr2:::validate_against_schema() accessible. script writes one .rda data/. running, update data-raw/_manifest.yaml.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Factor levels vs NA: "total" means “breakdown dimension”; NA means value unknown. Never use NA aggregates. Source keys: Always snake_case identifiers declared sources.yaml (e.g. kang_paese_felix_2021). Derived rows use composite keys like "source_a+source_b". Column names: English. PT-BR labels opt-via lang = "pt" resolved i18n.yaml query time — never baked stored data. is_derived: Flag rows computed combining components across sources. Excluded default (include_derived = FALSE) avoid double-counting. ETL dependencies: dplyr, tidyr, readxl Suggests (Imports) — needed data-raw/ scripts, end users.

title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: O educabr2 disponibiliza, sob um único esquema tidy canônico, o conjunto mais extenso de séries históricas harmonizadas sobre educação brasileira disponível em formato analítico: Matrículas ensino superior — 1907 2024, 118 anos de cobertura, articulando sete fontes primárias distintas (IBGE Estatísticas Século XX, Durham, Maduro Junior, Kang, INEP Sinopse, INEP Microdados e o painel CENSUP INEP). Matrículas ensino fundamental e médio — 1933 2010, com desagregação por cor/raça partir de 1960 (Kang, Paese & Felix 2021). Anos médios de escolaridade da população adulta — 1925 2015, com desagregação por sexo, cor/raça, macrorregião e unidade da federação (Walter & Kang 2024). Despesa pública em educação — 1933 2010, em % PIB por estágio, por estudante em % PIB per capita, e os indicadores de razão dupla de regressividade fiscal (Kang & Menetrier 2024). Razão de progressão escolar (GDR6) — 1955 2010, BR + 20 UFs, proxy da retenção nos primeiros anos antigo primário (Kang, Paese & Felix 2021). Toda transformação dos dados é auditável: cada linha output carrega o source (chave canônica em inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml) e um source_note com referência exata da tabela ou capítulo de origem.

what: Caso 1 — taxa bruta de matrícula por raça/cor
title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: Aqui usamos desagregação dimension = "race" para comparar trajetória de matrícula ensino fundamental entre os cinco grupos de cor/raça IBGE entre 1960 e 2010. diferença entre três principais categorias (white, black, brown) acompanha uma das narrativas centrais da literatura sociológica sobre educação Brasil — convergência relativa nas matrículas obrigatórias acompanhada de persistente desigualdade em níveis mais altos.
code: ef_raca <- get_enrollment(   level     = "fundamental",   indicator = "rate",   geo_level = "BR",   dimension = "race",   year      = c(1960, 2010) )  head(ef_raca) #> # A tibble: 6 × 16 #>    year geo_level geo_code geo_name level      network institution_type modality #>   <int> <chr>     <chr>    <chr>    <chr>      <chr>   <chr>            <chr>    #> 1  1960 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 2  1961 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 3  1962 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 4  1963 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 5  1964 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> 6  1965 BR        BR       Brasil   fundament… total   total            total    #> # ℹ 8 more variables: dim_race <chr>, age_group <chr>, indicator <chr>, #> #   value <dbl>, unit <chr>, source <chr>, source_note <chr>, is_derived <lgl>

what: Caso 2 — comparação multi-fonte no ensino superior
title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: O ensino superior brasileiro foi reconstituído por diversos autores com metodologias ligeiramente diferentes, sobretudo para o longo período 1933-2000 em que fontes oficiais são esparsas. O educabr2 mantém todas estimativas concorrentes lado lado, permitindo comparação direta: Note que para 1980 várias fontes convergem para o mesmo valor (~1,38 milhão), o que sugere uma estimativa estabilizada na literatura. Para outros anos estimativas divergem — passar source = "..." get_enrollment() é o caminho recomendado para fixar uma série única em análises secundárias.

what: Caso 3 — o problema dos totais reconstruídos (2000-2008)
title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: Entre 2000 e 2008 o INEP começou coletar matrículas em EAD (educação distância) em uma tabela específica CENSUP (tabela7.x), porém não somou ao total das matrículas presenciais publicadas na tabela5.x. Isso significa que séries “totais” desse intervalo publicadas por Kang, Durham, Maduro Junior e pela própria sinopse INEP estão sistematicamente subestimadas — em até 700 mil matrículas em 2008, segundo nosso cálculo. partir de 2009, os microdados CENSUP já agregam presencial e EAD mesmo cadastro, e o problema se resolve. Para esse intervalo de transição, o educabr2 disponibiliza totais reconstruídos (reconstructed enrollment totals) que somam o componente presencial de cada fonte ao EAD publicado pelo INEP. Essas linhas têm is_derived = TRUE e ficam fora resultado padrão de get_enrollment(); para inspecioná-las basta passar include_derived = TRUE: O nome composto source (ex.: kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup) explicita que se trata de uma combinação: o componente presencial vem da primeira fonte e o componente EAD vem da segunda. O source_note registra composição exata para fins de citação. Convenção de citação sugerida: “Total reconstruído computado partir de {fonte_presencial} (presencial) e INEP Sinopse CENSUP (EAD), conforme metodologia documentada em educabr2 (Mançano 2026).”
code: recon <- get_enrollment(   level           = "superior",   year            = c(2000, 2008),   network         = "total",   modality        = "total",   indicator       = "count",   include_derived = TRUE )  recon[recon$is_derived, c("year", "source", "value")] #> # A tibble: 23 × 3 #>     year source                                      value #>    <int> <chr>                                       <dbl> #>  1  2000 durham_2005+inep_sinopse_censup           2695927 #>  2  2000 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   2695927 #>  3  2000 kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup 2695927 #>  4  2000 maduro_junior_2007+inep_sinopse_censup    2695927 #>  5  2001 durham_2005+inep_sinopse_censup           3045113 #>  6  2001 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3036113 #>  7  2001 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3036113 #>  8  2001 kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup 3036113 #>  9  2001 maduro_junior_2007+inep_sinopse_censup    3036113 #> 10  2003 inep_sinopse_censup+inep_sinopse_censup   3936933 #> # ℹ 13 more rows

title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: série mostra inversão histórica gender gap: em 1925 os homens tinham em média mais anos de estudo que mulheres; ao longo século XX vantagem inverte e em 2015 mulheres ultrapassam homens em escolaridade média.

what: Caso 5 — regressividade fiscal na despesa educacional
title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: Kang & Menetrier (2024) operacionalizam uma tese clássica da economia política da educação brasileira — que o Estado gasta desproporcionalmente mais por aluno ensino superior que ensino primário — por meio indicador de razão dupla: despesa pública por estudante Ensino Superior dividida pela despesa por estudante EF1 (anos iniciais). Em 1933 o Estado gastava cerca de 66 vezes mais por aluno ES que por aluno dos primeiros anos primário. Em 2010 essa razão havia caído para menos de 9. série deixa explícito que expansão fiscal da educação básica ao longo da segunda metade século XX reduziu significativamente regressividade — embora não tenha eliminado.
code: razao <- get_expenditure(indicator = "double_ratio_es_ef1") razao[razao$year %in% c(1933, 1960, 1980, 2000, 2010),       c("year", "value")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>    year value #>   <int> <dbl> #> 1  1933 66.0  #> 2  1960 86.6  #> 3  1980 15.9  #> 4  2000 16.8  #> 5  2010  8.36

what: Caso 6 — retenção no início do primário (GDR6)
title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: O GDR6 (Gross Distribution Ratio da 6ª série) — definido como razão entre matrículas em séries 4–6 e matrículas em séries 1–3 antigo primário de oito anos — é um indicador de fluxo que mede quantas crianças avançam para além dos primeiros anos primário. Valores mais altos indicam menos evasão e menos repetência nos anos iniciais. O fosso persistente entre SP e BA é uma das ilustrações canônicas da desigualdade regional na literatura histórica: em 1960 diferença já é ampla, e só se estreita de forma substantiva depois das reformas dos anos 1990. Cobertura UF — atenção. compilação de Kang, Paese & Felix cobre 20 unidades da federação (AL, , BA, CE, ES, GO, MA, MG, MT, PA, PB, PE, PI, PR, RJ, RN, RS, SC, SE, SP). Os estados mais novos ou de origem territorial (AC, AP, DF, MS, RO, RR, ) não constam da fonte. série nacional brasileira tem buracos documentados em 1988-1990 e 1994, em razão das transições oficiais da estrutura de séries.
code: gdr_estados <- get_progression(   geo_level = "UF",   geo       = c("SP", "BA"),   year      = c(1955, 2010) )  gdr_estados[gdr_estados$year %in% c(1960, 1980, 2000, 2010),             c("year", "geo_code", "value")] #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>    year geo_code value #>   <int> <chr>    <dbl> #> 1  1960 BA       0.187 #> 2  1980 BA       0.276 #> 3  2000 BA       0.649 #> 4  2010 BA       0.848 #> 5  1960 SP       0.273 #> 6  1980 SP       0.665 #> 7  2000 SP       1.09  #> 8  2010 SP       0.952

title: Introdução ao educabr2 (PT-BR)
text: lista completa de fontes com DOIs e links está na vignette principal em inglês (vignette("introduction", "educabr2")). Para citar o pacote em si: Mançano, T. (2026). educabr2: Harmonized Historical Series Brazilian Education (versão 0.0.0.9000). https://github.com/mancano-tales/educabr

title: Introduction to educabr2
text: educabr2 provides, single canonical tidy schema, extensive set harmonised historical series Brazilian education available analytic format: Tertiary (higher-education) enrollment — 1907 2024, 118 years coverage, drawing seven distinct primary sources (IBGE Statistics 20th Century, Durham, Maduro Junior, Kang, INEP Synopsis, INEP Microdata, INEP CENSUP Power BI panel). Compulsory (fundamental) upper-secondary enrollment — 1933 2010, race/colour disaggregation 1960 onwards (Kang, Paese & Felix 2021). Mean years schooling adult population — 1925 2015, breakdowns sex, race/colour, macroregion state (Walter & Kang 2024). Public expenditure education — 1933 2010, share GDP stage, per-student spending share GDP per capita, Kang & Menetrier (2024) “double ratio” indicators fiscal regressivity. Grade-progression ratio (GDR6) — 1955 2010, BR + 20 UFs, proxy early-primary retention drawn Kang, Paese & Felix (2021). Every transformation auditable: output row carries canonical source key (catalogued inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml) source_note exact table chapter origin.

title: Introduction to educabr2
text: 2000 2008 INEP began collecting distance-learning (EAD) enrollment separate CENSUP table (tabela7.x), add total -person enrollment published tabela5.x. means headline “total” series interval published Kang, Durham, Maduro Junior, INEP’s synopsis systematically undercounted — ~700,000 enrollments 2008 reckoning. 2009 onwards CENSUP microdata already aggregate -person EAD registry, problem resolves . transition interval, educabr2 ships reconstructed totals combine -person component source INEP’s published EAD figures. rows carry is_derived = TRUE excluded default get_enrollment() output; pass include_derived = TRUE inspect : composite source key (e.g. kang_paese_felix_2021+inep_sinopse_censup) makes explicit combination: -person component comes first source EAD component comes second. source_note records exact composition citation purposes. what: Case 5 — fiscal regressivity in education spending
title: Introduction to educabr2
text: Kang & Menetrier (2024) operationalise long-standing claim political economy Brazilian education — State spends disproportionately per tertiary student per primary student — double ratio indicator: per-student public spending Ensino Superior divided per-student public spending EF1 (anos iniciais). 1933 State spent roughly 66 times per tertiary student per first-grade student. 2010 ratio fallen 9. series shows historical trajectory fiscal expansion basic education second half 20th century meaningfully reduce regressivity — though never eliminated .
code: ratio <- get_expenditure(indicator = "double_ratio_es_ef1") ratio[ratio$year %in% c(1933, 1960, 1980, 2000, 2010),       c("year", "value")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>    year value #>   <int> <dbl> #> 1  1933 66.0  #> 2  1960 86.6  #> 3  1980 15.9  #> 4  2000 16.8  #> 5  2010  8.36

title: Introduction to educabr2
text: GDR6 (Gross Distribution Ratio grade 6) — defined ratio enrollment grades 4–6 enrollment grades 1–3 old eight-year primary system — flow indicator many children make past early primary grades. Higher values mean fewer dropouts repeaters lower grades. persistent SP-vs-BA gap one canonical illustrations regional inequality historical literature: 1960 SP/BA ratio already wide, narrows substantially 1990s reforms. UF coverage caveat. Kang, Paese & Felix’s compilation covers 20 federation units (AL, , BA, CE, ES, GO, MA, MG, MT, PA, PB, PE, PI, PR, RJ, RN, RS, SC, SE, SP). Newer territorial-origin states (AC, AP, DF, MS, RO, RR, ) source. BR national series documented gaps 1988-1990 1994 due transitions official grade structure.
code: gdr_states <- get_progression(   geo_level = "UF",   geo       = c("SP", "BA"),   year      = c(1955, 2010) )  gdr_states[gdr_states$year %in% c(1960, 1980, 2000, 2010),            c("year", "geo_code", "value")] #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>    year geo_code value #>   <int> <chr>    <dbl> #> 1  1960 BA       0.187 #> 2  1980 BA       0.276 #> 3  2000 BA       0.649 #> 4  2010 BA       0.848 #> 5  1960 SP       0.273 #> 6  1980 SP       0.665 #> 7  2000 SP       1.09  #> 8  2010 SP       0.952

title: educabr2: Harmonised Brazilian Education Series
text: Harmonised historical series Brazilian education — enrollment educational attainment, compiled reconciled across decades heterogeneous official academic sources.  educabr2 provides curated long-run series Brazilian formal education — enrollment stage network, years schooling, literacy attainment — compiled reconciled heterogeneous official academic sources (Censo Escolar, PNAD, Censo Demográfico, Anuário Estatístico IBGE, INEP CENSUP, Kang/FGV-IBRE, Walter & Kang, among others) single tidy schema explicit per-row provenance. 🇧🇷 educabr2 reúne séries históricas tratadas sobre educação formal Brasil — matrículas, anos de estudo, atingimento educacional — em um único schema tidy, com proveniência explícita. Veja vinheta em português.

title: Enrollment series — Kang / FGV-IBRE 2023 compilation — enrollment_kang_fgv
text: tibble 6 238 rows 13 columns: year integer. Reference year observation (1871–2010). geo_level character. Geographic aggregation level: "BR" (national) "UF" (state). geo_code character. Geographic code: "BR" national; IBGE 2-letter UF abbreviation (e.g. "SP", "BA") states. geo_name character. Human-readable geographic name Portuguese (e.g. "Brasil", "São Paulo"). level character. Education stage using educabr2 vocabulary: "fundamental_anos_iniciais", "fundamental_anos_finais", "fundamental", "medio", "superior". network character. Administrative dependency. Always "total" source (network breakdown available). dim_race character. Race/colour dimension using educabr2 vocabulary ("white", "black", "brown", "asian", "indigenous"). "total" disaggregated. Race breakdown available BR level 1960–2010 . age_group character. Reference age bracket rate indicator (e.g. "7-14", "15-17", "18-24"). NA count indicators, headcounts tied population bracket. indicator character. Indicator key: "enrollment_count" (absolute headcount) "enrollment_rate" (gross enrollment rate, 0–100 scale). value double. Numeric value indicator. unit character. Unit measurement: "count" "percent". source character. Compact source key: "kang_fgv_ibre_2023". Full metadata inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml. source_note character. Inline bibliographic reference.

title: Brazilian school-enrollment series — get_enrollment
text: level Character vector one stage codes: "fundamental_anos_iniciais", "fundamental_anos_finais", "fundamental", "medio", "superior". NULL (default) means filter. network Character vector administrative-network codes ("federal", "estadual", "municipal", "publica", "privada", plus post-2009 private subcategories "privada_particular", "privada_comunitaria_confessional_filantropica", "privada_lucrativa", "privada_nao_lucrativa", "especial", "total"). NULL (default) means filter. institution_type Character vector restricting institutional category (meaningful level = "superior"). See inst/dict/schema.yaml controlled vocabulary. NULL (default) means filter; pass "total" keep rows aggregate across institutional types. modality Character vector: "presencial", "ead", "total". NULL (default) means filter. year Integer vector two-element c(min, max) range. NULL years. geo_level One "BR" (national, default) "UF" (state). geo Character vector 2-letter UF codes geo_level = "UF". NULL (default) returns UFs. dimension Inequality breakdown. One "none" (default, totals ) "race". Future versions add "sex", "income", "location". indicator Character vector. "count" counts, "rate" gross enrollment rates. NULL returns . source Character vector source keys (see inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml). NULL returns available sources. Tip: (year, level, network) covered multiple sources (common tertiary panel), pass source = "..." lock single series. include_derived Logical. FALSE (default), excludes -called reconstructed totals — rows value computed combining components different sources (typically -person enrollment single-source paper plus EAD enrollment INEP, 2000-2008 original sources -reported combined total). Set TRUE include . composition documented source_note; source column rows carries composite key "<presencial_source>+<ead_source>". effect datasets carry is_derived flag. wide Logical. TRUE, pivots indicator column wide form (one column per indicator). Default FALSE. lang One "en" (default) "pt". "pt", factor levels translated using inst/dict/i18n.yaml.

title: Brazilian public expenditure on education — get_expenditure
text: level Character vector stage codes: "fundamental_anos_iniciais", "fundamental_anos_finais", "fundamental", "medio", "fundamental_medio", "superior", "total". two "double ratio" indicators tagged level = "total". NULL (default) means filter. indicator Character vector. Convenience aliases accepted: "share_gdp", "per_student", "double_ratio_es_ef1", "double_ratio_es_ef_em". Full indicator keys ("expenditure_share_gdp", etc.) also work. NULL (default) returns four indicators. year Integer vector two-element c(min, max) range. NULL years. source Character vector source keys. NULL returns available sources (currently "kang_menetrier_2024"). wide Logical. TRUE, pivots result wide form (one column per indicator). Default FALSE. lang One "en" (default) "pt". "pt", factor levels indicator labels translated via inst/dict/i18n.yaml.

title: Brazilian public expenditure on education — get_expenditure
text: tibble canonical educabr2 long schema (see inst/dict/schema.yaml). Columns: year, geo_level, geo_code, geo_name, level, network, dim_race, age_group, indicator, value, unit, source, source_note. network always "publica" (public-sector expenditure ).

title: Brazilian public expenditure on education — get_expenditure
text: expenditure_share_gdp — total public expenditure given stage, share GDP. expenditure_per_student_pct_gdp_pc — per-student public expenditure public network, expressed share GDP per capita (unit-free, cross-country-comparable measure). expenditure_double_ratio_es_ef1 expenditure_double_ratio_es_ef_em — Kang & Menetrier's "double ratio" indicators fiscal regressivity (per-student spending tertiary divided per-student spending , respectively, EF1 EF+EM combined). series national (BR), 1933-2010. data behind function internal dataset expenditure_kang_fgv.

title: Brazilian public expenditure on education — get_expenditure
code: if (FALSE) { # Total public expenditure on education as share of GDP get_expenditure(indicator = "share_gdp", level = "total")  # Per-student spending in tertiary education over time get_expenditure(indicator = "per_student", level = "superior")  # The fiscal-regressivity "double ratio" — ES vs EF1 get_expenditure(indicator = "double_ratio_es_ef1")  # All indicators for 1933-1950 in wide form get_expenditure(year = c(1933, 1950), wide = TRUE) }

title: Brazilian grade-progression series — get_progression
text: Returns harmonised series grade-progression indicators Brazilian school system. bundled indicator GDR6 (Gross Distribution Ratio grade 6), defined enrollment grades 4-6 old eight-year primary system divided enrollment grades 1-3. Higher values indicate fewer drop-outs repeaters early grades primary education.

Coverage note: source covers 20 UFs, 27 — newer / territorial-origin federation units (AC, AP, DF, MS, RO, RR, ) Kang's compilation. Passing one codes emits warning explaining gap returns remaining (covered) UFs . source Character vector source keys. NULL returns available sources (currently "kang_paese_felix_2021"). wide Logical. text: year Integer vector two-element c(min, max) range. NULL years. geo_level One "BR" (national, default), "region" (macro-region), "UF" (state). Region UF series start 1950. geo Character vector geographic codes. geo_level = "UF", 2-letter IBGE UF abbreviations (e.g. "SP", "BA"). geo_level = "region", one "N", "NE", "CO", "SE", "S". NULL (default) returns geographies level. dimension Inequality breakdown. One : "none" (default) — national totals (race sex split); "race" — breakdown IBGE color/race (white, black, brown, asian, indigenous), totals across sex; "sex" — breakdown sex (male, female), totals across race. Race sub-national breakdowns available geo_level = "BR". source Character vector source keys. NULL returns available sources (currently "walter_kang_2023"). wide Logical. TRUE, pivots result wide form. indicator effect minimal (one indicator column), parameter provided API consistency get_enrollment(). Default FALSE. lang One "en" (default) "pt". "pt", factor levels translated via inst/dict/i18n.yaml.

what: List the data sources bundled with educabr2 — list_sources
title: List the data sources bundled with educabr2 — list_sources
text: Returns tibble describing every entry controlled source vocabulary (inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml). One row per source key, carrying short name, type, temporal/geographic coverage, DOI, URL free-text notes. Use discover sources available calling educabr_cite() filtering get_enrollment() / get_schooling() source argument.

title: List the data sources bundled with educabr2 — list_sources
code: list_sources()

title: List the data sources bundled with educabr2 — list_sources
text: tibble columns: key source key used source column get_enrollment() / get_schooling() output. short_name compact human-readable label. type one "academic", "academic_thesis", "official_survey", "census", "administrative", "administrative_microdata", "historical_compilation". year_start, year_end integer; year_end NA ongoing series. geo character; comma-separated list geographic levels covered ("BR", "region", "UF"). doi character; NA source none. url stable URL source. notes free-text remarks YAML.

Revista de Historia Económica, 39(2), 191–218. doi:10.1017/S0212610921000112 . Compilation: FGV/IBRE (April 2023 revision). ETL script: data-raw/05_build_progression_kang_fgv.R.

title: educabr2 0.1.0
text: First public release. Initial set harmonised long-run series Brazilian education plus bundled Shiny dashboard.

title: educabr2 0.1.0
text: enrollment_kang_fgv — 6,238 rows. Brazilian school enrollment counts gross rates stage (EF1, EF2, EF, EM, ES), 1933-2010 national level 1955-2010 UF level, breakdown colour/race 1960-2010. Per-paper source attribution (kang_paese_felix_2021, kang_menetrier_2024, kang_menetrier_comim_2024). enrollment_tertiary — 1,341 rows. Brazilian tertiary enrollment 1907-2024 compiled across seven primary sources: IBGE Estatísticas Século XX, Durham (2005), Maduro Junior (2007), Kang/Paese/Felix (2021), INEP CENSUP Synopsis (1995-2008), INEP CENSUP Microdata (2009-2024), INEP CENSUP Power BI panel. Multiple sources per year-network kept purpose support cross-source comparison. Includes 25 reconstructed total rows (is_derived = TRUE) fill 2000-2008 transition period INEP published -person EAD enrollment separately. schooling_kang_fgv — 2,287 rows. Mean years schooling adult population, 1925-2015 (BR), 1950-2015 (region, UF), sex race breakdowns BR level (Walter & Kang 2024).

title: educabr2 0.1.0
text: get_enrollment() — long-format access enrollment series filters level, network, institution_type, modality, year, geo_level/geo, dimension, indicator, source, include_derived. Returns canonical schema English labels (lang = "en") PT-BR labels (lang = "pt"). get_schooling() — long-format access mean-years--schooling series filters year, geo_level/geo, dimension, source, lang. run_dashboard() — launches bundled Shiny dashboard locally. educabr_cite() — builds bibentry objects (APA-style prose, BibTeX) harmonised data sources, driven controlled vocabulary inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml. list_sources() — returns tibble describing every entry source vocabulary (key, type, temporal/geographic coverage, DOI, URL). Discovery counterpart educabr_cite().

title: educabr2 0.1.0
text: Three-theme navbar (English UI): Enrollment, Tertiary Education, Educational Attainment. Tertiary Education tab features multi-source comparison interaction-based colour palette (source × modality combination gets unique colour shade), shape--source, linetype--modality encoding. “View R code” button every tab generates self-contained, copy-pasteable R snippet (educabr2 + ggplot2) reproduces current chart locally.

title: educabr2 0.1.0
text: Canonical tidy-long schema documented inst/dict/schema.yaml primary-key constraints, year domain, controlled vocabularies factor levels, conventions missing values. 13 primary source entries documented inst/dict/vocabularies/sources.yaml DOIs, URLs, coverage metadata. PT-BR labels every factor level inst/dict/i18n.yaml.
